title: The One with Ross's New Girlfriend

jokes.text: Rachel frantically rushing through airport saying 'Emergency, please!' to get to Ross
jokes.setup: Rachel is desperate to confess her feelings to Ross at the airport
jokes.punchline: Her over-the-top urgency for what's actually a romantic mission
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central tension with Rachel's comedic desperation. Well-integrated character moment.

jokes.text: Rachel bleeding from her rush to airport, trying to play it cool: 'Enough about me, Mr. Back From The Orient!'
jokes.setup: Rachel is literally bleeding but trying to act casual
jokes.punchline: Her awkward deflection with 'Mr. Back From The Orient' while injured
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rachel - trying to be casual while literally falling apart. The outdated 'Orient' reference adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Rachel giving flowers meant for Ross to Julie: 'Welcome to our country.' / Julie: 'I'm from New York.'
jokes.setup: Rachel awkwardly gives Julie flowers with xenophobic assumption
jokes.punchline: Julie is also American, making Rachel's gesture absurdly misplaced
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline structure. Rachel's desperation leads to casually racist assumption that gets immediately deflated.

jokes.text: Rachel's recovery: 'Not a problem. We'll use them to stop the bleeding.'
jokes.setup: Rachel's flower gesture just backfired embarrassingly
jokes.punchline: She pivots to practical use while acknowledging her injury matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Nice character recovery moment showing Rachel's quick thinking under pressure.

jokes.text: 'This is bigger than huge.' / 'What's bigger than huge?' / 'Um, this?'
jokes.setup: Monica searching for word bigger than 'huge'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal pointing gesture as if size could be demonstrated
jokes.explanation: Simple verbal comedy. Works but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: 'Thanks to Vidal Buffay' / 'If you don't look good, we don't look good'
jokes.setup: Phoebe cut their hair
jokes.punchline: Parody of Vidal Sassoon with her surname + the salon slogan
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining Phoebe's quirky character with recognizable commercial parody. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Phoebe miming/acting out the airport scene: 'Airport. Ross. Not alone. Julie. Arm around her!' then fake cramping
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to convey bad news about Ross
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic physical performance ending in fake medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her theatrical communication style makes bad news delivery comedically dramatic.

jokes.text: Chandler: 'I think she's trying to tell us something. Quick, get the verbs.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe is clearly communicating through charades
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mock-helpful suggestion about her telegraphic speech
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler wit - economical, sharp observation delivered with perfect deadpan timing. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Julie: 'I'm not here, you haven't met me. I'll make a better impression when I don't have 20 hours of plane on me.'
jokes.setup: Julie meeting Ross's friends for first time
jokes.punchline: Self-aware about her disheveled state, asking for a do-over
jokes.explanation: Likeable character moment that makes Julie relatable rather than threatening.

jokes.text: Ross and Julie telling travel story, Joey interrupts: 'And the chicken poops in her lap!'
jokes.setup: Ross building suspense about their travel story
jokes.punchline: Joey ruins the story by blurting out the punchline
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey move - impulsive spoiling of stories. Good structure with buildup and deflation.

jokes.text: Rachel: 'Isn't that just kick-you-in-the-crotch, spit-on-your-neck fantastic?'
jokes.setup: Monica asks if Ross meeting Julie isn't great
jokes.punchline: Rachel's violently specific fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the specific violent imagery makes Rachel's jealousy hilariously clear while maintaining plausible deniability.

jokes.text: Rachel's follow-up: 'It's an expression.'
jokes.setup: Everyone's confused by her violent 'enthusiasm'
jokes.punchline: Rachel doubles down, pretending it's a real saying
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that enhances the original joke. Rachel's commitment to the bit makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Monica about future plans: 'Don't tell us what's gonna happen though, because I like to be surprised.'
jokes.setup: Ross mentions their schedule
jokes.punchline: Monica treats mundane plans like spoilers for entertainment
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about treating real life like entertainment. Works but not standout.

jokes.text: Ross asking for coffee, Rachel: 'Did you talk to him yet?' Chandler: 'No.' Rachel: 'Then, no.'
jokes.setup: Rachel's clearly been waiting to hear about Chandler's conversation
jokes.punchline: Her conditional service based on information she wants
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's desperation for information. Economical joke structure.

jokes.text: Ross: 'You know whose voice that was?' Chandler: 'God?' Ross: 'It was you, pal.'
jokes.setup: Ross asks about the voice telling him to move on
jokes.punchline: Chandler guesses 'God' but it was just Chandler himself
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection with Chandler's self-important guess deflated by mundane reality.

jokes.text: Chandler: 'Well, maybe it was God doing me.'
jokes.setup: Learning he was Ross's 'inner voice'
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests he might be God's vessel, self-aggrandizing
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - turning self-deprecation into self-importance with clever wordplay.

jokes.text: Rachel bringing wrong drink, insisting Ross return lemonade he didn't order to avoid charges
jokes.setup: Rachel is eavesdropping and makes serving mistake
jokes.punchline: Her insistence on Ross correcting her error for financial reasons
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's distraction and desperation. Competent character comedy but not remarkable.

jokes.text: Chandler about silver lining: 'You have to really wanna see it.'
jokes.setup: Rachel confused about how Ross choosing Julie himself is a silver lining
jokes.punchline: Chandler admits his optimism requires serious mental gymnastics
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler moment - acknowledging the absurdity of forced optimism with perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Chandler about guys working out: 'Ironically, these guys were picked last in gym.'
jokes.setup: Seeing fit guys exercising
jokes.punchline: Ironic observation about former outcasts becoming athletic
jokes.explanation: Standard observational humor with ironic twist. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Phoebe mind-reading: 'How it's been so long since you've had sex, you're wondering if they've changed it?'
jokes.setup: Monica asks Phoebe to guess what she's thinking
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's inappropriately specific sexual assumption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe moment - unexpectedly specific and forward, treating sex like a software update. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Monica: 'No. Although now that's what I'm thinking.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe's inappropriate mind-reading
jokes.punchline: Monica admits Phoebe planted the idea in her head
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that enhances the original joke. Monica's honesty makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Monica about haircut control: 'I'm incredibly anal and an unbelievable control freak.' / Phoebe: 'No, you're not.' / Monica: 'I know I'm not, but you are.'
jokes.setup: Monica seeming to have self-awareness about her control issues
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was actually insulting Phoebe while 'sparing feelings'
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Monica's passive-aggressive nature. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Chandler looking for tailor: 'I'm just looking for a man to draw on me with chalk.'
jokes.setup: Joey asks if he needs clothes altered
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deliberately literal description of tailoring
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - taking normal activity and making it sound weird through literal description.

jokes.text: Joey's age confusion: 'He did my first suit when I was 15. No wait, 16. No, excuse me, 15.' / 'When was 1990?'
jokes.setup: Joey trying to remember when he got his first suit
jokes.punchline: He can't do basic math to figure out his age in a specific year
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment showing his mathematical incompetence. Very character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Chandler: 'You have to stop the Q-tip when there's resistance.'
jokes.setup: Non-sequitur advice
jokes.punchline: Chandler's random wisdom about ear cleaning
jokes.explanation: Random Chandler observation. Quotable but lacks clear setup or strong punchline.

jokes.text: Ross and Julie's phone call: Extended 'no you hang up' routine ending with 'She didn't hang up'
jokes.setup: Typical couple phone ending ritual
jokes.punchline: The awkward reality that neither actually hung up despite the performance
jokes.explanation: Extended cringe comedy showing new couple behavior. Relatable but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Chandler interrupting phone call: 'Sorry, I thought you were talking to me.'
jokes.setup: Ross doing baby talk on phone
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry acknowledgment of the confusing intimacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler timing and delivery. Good reaction to the cringe of couple talk.

jokes.text: Monica: 'I don't care what you guys say. Something's bothering her.'
jokes.setup: Rachel just had a complete emotional breakdown and ran out
jokes.punchline: Monica's massive understatement about Rachel's obvious distress
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor. Monica stating the obvious after dramatic exit works well.

jokes.text: Joey: 'You know, I think I was 16.'
jokes.setup: Joey was confused about his age earlier
jokes.punchline: He's still thinking about it much later, arriving at wrong conclusion
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Joey's continued confusion. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Monica about haircut: 'No, no, no. I'm not falling for that again.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks Monica to close her eyes
jokes.punchline: Implies Phoebe has tricked her before with this request
jokes.explanation: Suggests history between characters. Works to build Phoebe's mischievous character.

jokes.text: Visual reveal of Paolo in Monica's apartment
jokes.setup: Monica mentions doing something stupid
jokes.punchline: Paolo appears, explaining the 'stupid thing'
jokes.explanation: Effective visual reveal. Monica's desperation made manifest.

jokes.text: Monica: 'I'm pathetic, I'm a loser.' / Rachel: 'Honey, you're not pathetic. You're just sad.'
jokes.setup: Monica being self-deprecating
jokes.punchline: Rachel's 'comforting' distinction that doesn't really help
jokes.explanation: Good friend dynamic - Rachel's attempt at comfort that isn't really comforting.

jokes.text: Phoebe: 'My God, if I had a nickel for every guy I wish I hadn't...' Monica: 'But this is about your horrible mistake.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe starting to share her own experiences
jokes.punchline: Monica redirecting back to her own problems with blunt honesty
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Monica's self-centeredness and Phoebe's oversharing tendency.

jokes.text: Ross and Julie: 'We were, uh... Well, there was touching.'
jokes.setup: They're late to the apartment
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward euphemism for why they're delayed
jokes.explanation: Ross's characteristic awkwardness about sexual topics. 'Touching' is a funny euphemism.

jokes.text: Paolo: 'I do Raquel.' / Ross: 'Ah.'
jokes.setup: Paolo explaining why he's there
jokes.punchline: His broken English makes innocent hairdressing sound sexual + Ross's awkward reaction
jokes.explanation: Double entendre from language barrier. Ross's discomfort adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Monica: 'You're gonna have to not touch my ass.'
jokes.setup: Paolo is being inappropriately handsy
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact boundary setting
jokes.explanation: Monica's direct approach to uncomfortable situation. The casual delivery makes it funny.

jokes.text: Chandler: 'Well, despite the yummy bagels and palpable tension...'
jokes.setup: Awkward situation with Paolo and Ross in same room
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation of the uncomfortable atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - acknowledging awkwardness while maintaining light tone. 'Palpable tension' is nicely specific.

jokes.text: Joey about Frankie: 'He'll know what it means.' / Chandler: 'You sure he's gonna be able to crack that code?'
jokes.setup: Joey says to tell Frankie that 'Joey Tribbiani says hello'
jokes.punchline: Chandler mocks the complexity of this 'coded' message
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler wit - pointing out absurdity of Joey treating a simple greeting like secret code. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: Monica about haircut: 'The last time Paolo was here, my hair was so much shorter and cuter.'
jokes.setup: Paolo's presence triggering memories
jokes.punchline: Monica focusing on superficial hair comparison rather than relationship issues
jokes.explanation: Very Monica to focus on appearance details during emotional moment. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Phoebe to hair: 'Some of you are gonna get cut and some of you aren't. But I promise, none of you will feel a thing.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe about to cut Monica's hair
jokes.punchline: She addresses the hair directly like it's sentient beings she's about to execute
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird Phoebe moment - treating hair like conscious beings with dark surgery undertones. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Monica: 'This isn't the face of a person who trusts a person. This is the face of a person who doesn't trust a person.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe says Monica doesn't trust her based on her expression
jokes.punchline: Monica's overly analytical breakdown of her own facial expression
jokes.explanation: Very Monica to over-analyze and explain obvious things. Verbose but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Phoebe: 'This is how he wears it.' / Monica: 'How who wears it?' / Phoebe: 'Demi Moore.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe defending her haircut choice
jokes.punchline: She refers to female actress Demi Moore as 'he'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Demi/Dudley Moore confusion. Phoebe's gender confusion is believable character mistake.

jokes.text: Demi/Dudley Moore mix-up: 'That's Dudley Moore!' / 'I said I wanted it like Demi Moore!'
jokes.setup: Confusion over similar-sounding names
jokes.punchline: Phoebe confused completely different people - male comedian vs female actress
jokes.explanation: Classic mix-up comedy. The similar names make it believable that Phoebe would confuse them.

jokes.text: Tailor measuring pants: 'How long do you want the cuffs?' / Chandler: 'At least as long as I have the pants.'
jokes.setup: Normal tailoring question about cuff length
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal interpretation suggesting permanent attachment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler wordplay - taking normal question and making it absurd through literal interpretation. Economical and clever.

jokes.text: Chandler: 'I just got that.'
jokes.setup: Chandler just made his own joke
jokes.punchline: He's slow to recognize his own wit
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment where character acknowledges his own joke. Very economical.

jokes.text: Ross about Monica's hair: 'Your hair looks too good. It would upset her.'
jokes.setup: Others want to see Monica after bad haircut
jokes.punchline: Ross's logic about good hair being insensitive to someone with bad hair
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about hair-related sensitivity. Ross showing unexpected social awareness.

jokes.text: Joey: 'Look, I can sense when women are depressed and vulnerable. It's one of my gifts.'
jokes.setup: Joey trying to comfort Monica
jokes.punchline: He frames predatory behavior as a supernatural ability
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Joey moment - he's completely oblivious to how creepy this sounds. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Monica: 'Today it's like there's rock bottom, 50 feet of crap, then me.'
jokes.setup: Monica thought hitting rock bottom was bad
jokes.punchline: She's found an even lower level of despair with specific measurements
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke with specific visual imagery. Very quotable and relatable description of despair.

jokes.text: Joey: 'I've been with a lot of people's share of women.'
jokes.setup: Joey claiming experience with women
jokes.punchline: Suggests he's taken other people's portions, like a buffet
jokes.explanation: Great Joey line treating romantic relationships like distributed resources. Clever wordplay.

jokes.text: Joey: 'Your tailor is a very bad man!'
jokes.setup: Joey storms in upset about tailor visit
jokes.punchline: His dramatic proclamation about what should be routine service
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the tailor misunderstanding. Joey's dramatic delivery sells the outrage.

jokes.text: Chandler about measuring: 'Cupping.'
jokes.setup: Chandler describing what the tailor did
jokes.punchline: His euphemistic term for inappropriate touching
jokes.explanation: Perfect word choice - 'cupping' is both euphemistic and oddly specific. Great economy.

jokes.text: Ross: 'Yes, yes, it is. In prison!'
jokes.setup: Chandler asks if that's how tailors measure pants
jokes.punchline: Ross confirms but adds alarming context about where this happens
jokes.explanation: Excellent punchline that completely recontextualizes the situation. Perfect timing and delivery indication.

jokes.text: Monica about hair: 'Even Mary Tyler Moore would've been better.'
jokes.setup: Monica's haircut is terrible
jokes.punchline: She wishes they'd confused Demi Moore with a different, safer choice
jokes.explanation: Callback to the name confusion. Mary Tyler Moore would indeed be safer hair choice than Dudley Moore.

jokes.text: Phoebe: 'I think it's a 10!' / Monica: 'Thank you. My hair is very amused.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe giving Monica's bad haircut a perfect score
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic response treating hair like it has feelings
jokes.explanation: Nice sarcastic response from Monica. 'Amused' is good word choice for the situation.

jokes.text: Christopher Cross reference: 'You could get caught between the moon and New York City. I know it's crazy, but it's true.'
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to cheer Monica up
jokes.punchline: Random obscure song lyric reference that doesn't help at all
jokes.explanation: Dated reference that may not land with all audiences. Chandler's randomness is in character but joke is weak.

jokes.text: Ross leaving: 'When the doctor does that hernia test...' Chandler: 'That's okay.'
jokes.setup: Ross about to share more inappropriate touching concerns
jokes.punchline: Chandler cutting him off before TMI
jokes.explanation: Good callback to tailor situation. Chandler's preemptive shutdown is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Ross about Paolo: 'I hate him. I mean, I actually... I physically hate him.'
jokes.setup: Ross expressing dislike for Paolo
jokes.punchline: The escalation from emotional to physical hatred
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing intensity of Ross's feelings. The physical specificity is funny.

jokes.text: Ross's romantic speech ending with: 'Like the way I do with Julie.'
jokes.setup: Ross giving beautiful speech about what Rachel deserves
jokes.punchline: He concludes by referencing his current relationship, crushing Rachel
jokes.explanation: Devastating misdirection. Perfect setup makes the deflation even more painful. Excellent cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Chandler on phone: 'No, I swear to God, Dad! That's not how they measure pants!'
jokes.setup: Tailor situation has traumatized Chandler
jokes.punchline: He's now calling his father to complain about proper measuring technique
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows the lasting impact. Chandler seeking validation from father is perfect character touch.

jokes.text: Final Andie/Roddy MacDowell confusion: 'That's Roddy McDowall. Andie MacDowell is the guy from Planet of the Apes.'
jokes.setup: Another celebrity name confusion for haircut
jokes.punchline: Phoebe has them completely backwards - male actor from Planet of the Apes vs female actress
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier confusion. Phoebe's complete reversal is believable for her character. Strong ending joke.

tmdb_overview: Rachel waits patiently at the airport for Ross, returning from China, while Monica gets haircuts and Joey visits his tailor.
